affected_layers: UI – Astro component CSS and HTML attribute
problem_statement: The Julian Kusi logo in the 'Plan Your Expedition with a Free Consultation' CTA section renders at 200 px, which is too small relative to the surrounding content.
solution_strategy: Increase logo width from 200 px to 280 px in both the HTML width attribute and the CSS .cta-logo rule. Slightly enhance the drop-shadow spread to remain proportionate to the new size.

changes.description: Updated HTML width attribute from '200' to '280' and height attribute from '60' to '84' (preserves 10:3 aspect ratio)
Updated .cta-logo CSS width from 200px to 280px; slightly increased drop-shadow spread from 12px to 16px for proportional glow

phase_3_component.red: Visually confirm 200px logo looks undersized in CTA section
phase_3_component.green: Apply width: 280px to .cta-logo CSS and width='280' HTML attribute
phase_3_component.refactor: Verify layout integrity on desktop (≥901px) and confirm mobile media query still hides .cta-deco

phase_4_integration.component_rendering: No new wiring required; img already rendered inside .cta-deco div
phase_4_integration.user_discovery: Logo visible on desktop when user scrolls to CTA section

acceptance_criteria: src/components/CTA.astro updated with width='280' attribute and .cta-logo { width: 280px }
Astro build succeeds with no errors
Logo visually larger and aesthetically balanced on desktop
Mobile layout (≤900px) unaffected – logo column remains hidden
assumptions: The logo.webp file has sufficient resolution to look crisp at 280px width
The two-column CTA grid has enough space for a 280px logo column at typical desktop widths (≥901px)
